name: skill-graduation
description: Take a skill through the full graduation pipeline. Evaluate quality, generate test cases, run shadow validation against reference outputs, check health metrics, improve weak areas, and promote to graduated status. The meta-chain for skill quality assurance.

config.instruction: Read the skill.md file provided. Extract: skill name, execution pattern (ORPA/pipeline), inputs, outputs, number of phases, error handling table, exit criteria, and any quality gates. Assess completeness: does it have all required sections? Are inputs/outputs clearly typed? Are quality gates specific and measurable? Output: skill_analysis with completeness_score (0-1) and missing_sections list.

Based on the skill analysis, generate 5 test cases for shadow validation. Each test case should have: (1) a realistic input context (JSON), (2) expected output structure (what fields should be present), (3) quality criteria (what makes the output good vs bad), (4) edge case being tested (normal, missing input, ambiguous input, large input, adversarial input). These test cases will be used by validators to shadow-execute the skill and compare outputs.

Review the skill based on the analysis. Fix any missing sections, strengthen weak quality gates, add error handling for uncovered failure modes, improve phase descriptions for clarity. Output the improved skill.md content. Don't change the core functionality — just make it more robust and complete.

Run shadow validation: execute the improved skill against each of the 5 test cases. For each test case: (1) provide the test input as context, (2) execute the skill following its instructions, (3) compare your output against the expected structure, (4) score similarity (0-1) based on field presence, value reasonableness, and structural match. Output: validation_results with per-test scores and an overall pass_rate. A test passes if similarity >= 0.75.

Based on the shadow validation results, produce a graduation decision. Criteria: (1) At least 4 of 5 tests pass (80% pass rate), (2) No test scores below 0.5, (3) Skill has all required sections (from analysis), (4) Error handling covers at least 3 failure modes. Output: graduation_status (GRADUATED / NEEDS_WORK / REJECTED), overall_score, recommendations for improvement if not graduated, and a quality badge level (Bronze / Silver / Gold based on avg similarity score).
